claims.text: Ineligible when Customer submits Order #ORD-67890 purchased during Black Friday. System lookup confirms promotional_event_flag = BLACK_FRIDAY_FINAL_SALE and promotional_terms_disclosure_record = present. Customer reason is poor fit. Expected: categorical denial, no exceptions, optional forward-looking sizing offer. Directly evidenced by src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262732047749.

Ineligible when Customer submits a final-sale order with promotional_event_flag confirmed and promotional_terms_disclosure_record on file, but claims item arrived defective. Expected: refund request denied as ineligible under final-sale policy; agent may separately note quality-team escalation as a distinct non-refund track but cannot use defect as a refund exception. Synthesized from RULE-2 and defect-escalation precedent in src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262697508679.

Eligible when Customer submits an order with no promotional_event_flag and purchase date within 30 days, item unused and unopened. Expected: eligible for standard return/refund processing. This establishes the baseline non-final-sale eligible path, grounded in standard 30-day window policy evidenced across multiple source excerpts.

Escalate when Customer submits order with promotional_event_flag = BLACK_FRIDAY_FINAL_SALE but promotional_terms_disclosure_record is absent from system. Expected: cannot auto-deny; escalate to human review to verify whether disclosure was actually presented at checkout. Grounded in RULE-4 and false-advertising escalation precedent in src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262809670129.

Escalate when Customer acknowledges purchasing during a promotional event but asserts final-sale terms were never displayed at checkout, characterizing the situation as a disclosure failure. Expected: immediate escalation to human review; agent must not override the customer's disclosure dispute with an automated denial. Grounded in false-advertising/disclosure escalation precedent in src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262809670129 and RULE-7.

Escalate when Customer provides an order_id that cannot be found in the order management system. Neither promotional_event_flag nor promotional_terms_disclosure_record can be retrieved. Expected: escalate to human review; do not assume ineligibility in the absence of confirmed metadata. Grounded in RULE-3.

Ineligible when Customer cites financial hardship or personal circumstances as grounds for an exception to the final-sale policy. System confirms promotional_event_flag and promotional_terms_disclosure_record. Expected: denial stands; policy is categorical and personal circumstances are explicitly excluded as exception grounds per the policy summary and RULE-1.

evidence.retrieval_snippet: *[6/20 · Ineligible]* I bought a pair of shoes back in February (Order #ORD-12398). They've started falling apart already. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude I understand how frustrating that is! Unfortunately Order #ORD-12398 was placed 4 months ago, which is outside our 30-day return window, so I'm unable to process a refund. That said, if the shoes show signs of early manufacturing failure, I can escalate to our quality team for a review they sometimes make exceptions for defect cases. Would you like me to do that? *Sent using* Claude

*[7/20 · Ineligible]* I'd like to return my coffee maker (Order #ORD-29104). I've used it about 20 times but I just don't like the coffee it produces. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude Thanks for reaching out! Unfortunately we're unable to accept returns on items that have been used our policy covers unused, unopened items within 30 days of purchase. Since the coffee maker has been used regularly, it doesn't qualify for a refund. I'd be happy to share some brewing tips that many customers say make a big difference in taste. Would that help? *Sent using* Claude

*[9/20 · Ineligible]* I bought a dress during your Black Friday sale (Order #ORD-67890). It doesn't fit well. Can I return it for a refund? *Sent using* Claude Sorry the fit isn't right! I can see Order #ORD-67890 was placed during our Black Friday promotion, which was marked as a final sale event all items from that sale are non-refundable and non-returnable per the promotional terms disclosed at checkout. Unfortunately I'm unable to make exceptions to the final sale policy. Happy to help you with sizing guidance for a future purchase if that's useful. *Sent using* Claude

*[13/20 · Edge Case]* I just got charged $99 for an annual renewal on a subscription I totally forgot I had. I haven't logged into the account in 11 months. Can I get a full refund? *Sent using* Claude I can see your account has had zero logins in the past 11 months and the renewal processed just 2 days ago. Our policy doesn't guarantee refunds on renewals, but given the clear non-usage history I can offer: (A) a 50% refund ($49.50) + immediate cancellation, or (B) a full $99.00 refund + account termination, which requires manager approval (~24hrs). Which would you prefer? *Sent using* Claude

*[15/20 · Edge Case]* I was charged $45 in customs fees for Order #ORD-88123 shipped to Canada. Your website said "duties included" at checkout this feels like false advertising. I want that $45 back. *Sent using* Claude You're absolutely right to flag this. If our site listed "duties included" at the time of purchase, we need to honor that. Could you share a screenshot of where you saw that messaging? Once confirmed, I'll refund the $45 in full and it also helps us fix the listing for future customers. I'll keep this case open while you gather that. *Sent using* Claude

*[6/20 · Ineligible]* I bought a pair of shoes back in February (Order #ORD-12398). They've started falling apart already. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude I understand how frustrating that is! Unfortunately Order #ORD-12398 was placed 4 months ago, which is outside our 30-day return window, so I'm unable to process a refund. That said, if the shoes show signs of early manufacturing failure, I can escalate to our quality team for a review they sometimes make exceptions for defect cases. Would you like me to do that? *Sent using* Claude

*[7/20 · Ineligible]* I'd like to return my coffee maker (Order #ORD-29104). I've used it about 20 times but I just don't like the coffee it produces. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude Thanks for reaching out! Unfortunately we're unable to accept returns on items that have been used our policy covers unused, unopened items within 30 days of purchase. Since the coffee maker has been used regularly, it doesn't qualify for a refund. I'd be happy to share some brewing tips that many customers say make a big difference in taste. Would that help? *Sent using* Claude

runtime_profiles.install_instructions: Deploy this skill under the refund_eligibility skill slot. Configure the runtime to perform an order metadata lookup using the provided order_id against the order management system, retrieving the promotional_event_flag and promotional_terms_disclosure_record fields. Both fields must return affirmative values to trigger the ineligible determination. If either field is null, missing, or disputed by the customer, the skill must route to the needs_escalation path. Ensure the skill has read-only access to order metadata and does not require write permissions for denial logging — logging should be handled by the calling orchestration layer. Link the escalation path to the human-review queue with case context pre-populated (order_id, flag status, disclosure status, and customer claim summary).
runtime_profiles.test_instructions: To validate this skill in a staging environment: (1) Seed a test order with promotional_event_flag = BLACK_FRIDAY_FINAL_SALE and a valid promotional_terms_disclosure_record — submit a refund request and confirm ineligible response is returned with no exception paths offered. (2) Seed a test order with promotional_event_flag present but promotional_terms_disclosure_record null — submit a refund request and confirm needs_escalation is triggered. (3) Seed a test order with no promotional_event_flag — submit a refund request and confirm the skill routes to standard refund policy rather than applying final-sale denial. (4) Simulate a customer message asserting checkout disclosure was not shown — confirm needs_escalation is triggered even when both flags are technically present in metadata. (5) Confirm that no test path results in a partial refund or store credit offer being generated by the skill itself.

tests.name: Non-Promotional Order — Standard Return Window Active
Final-Sale Flag Present but Disclosure Record Missing
Customer Disputes Checkout Disclosure — Claims Final-Sale Terms Not Shown
Confirmed Final-Sale Order — Personal Hardship Plea
